We recommended that the:
a. MSWDO and MDRRMO (i) provide justification on the deficiencies noted in
the distribution of welfare goods; (ii) submit to the Audit Team the lacking
distribution lists pertaining to the procured welfare goods, if any; (iii) effect
complete accounting for and reconcile the noted difference in the quantities
of procured and distributed relief items; and (iv) ensure that welfare goods
distributed are properly accounted and tallied with the record of its
procurement;
b. MHO provide justification for the deficiencies noted in the liquidation of the
COVID-19 Benefit grant and ensure the veracity of documents before
payments are made;
c. Municipal Accountant ensure that income from grants received by the
Municipality and disbursements relative thereto are properly recognized and
classified in the books of account for fair presentation, transparency and
accountability of the funds utilized; and
d. Concerned Municipal Officials (i) comment on the observations noted on the
procurement process undertaken by the Municipality during the COVID-19
situation, (ii) submit the lacking documents for audit purposes, and (iii)
ensure that future claims are supported with complete supporting documents
in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

In a letter dated March 22, 2022, the Municipal Accountant committed that they
will adhere to and comply with the necessary requirements for the procurement through
emergency cases.

The Municipality submitted the undated MOA between them and GBH Power
Resources, Inc. and the Annual Work Program for evaluation by the Audit Team.

In a letter dated March 21, 2022, the MSWDO committed to double-check their
file copies relative to the unreconciled quantities of distributed welfare goods and submit
the tallied beneficiaries based on the RDS to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer (i) ensure reconciliation of their respective records relative to cash
accounts, (ii) conduct a detailed review and checking of bank records/reports and
request the necessary documents to support the recording of the reconciling items, if
any, (iii) exert continuous effort to substantiate the identified reconciling items and
effect the necessary adjustments to corresponding records, if warranted, (iv)
provide details of the outstanding checks in the BRS for easy monitoring of its
validity, and (v) review the long outstanding/stale checks and ensure compliance
with the procedures as provided under the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.

In a letter dated March 22, 2022, the Municipal Accountant informed the Audit
Team that the unreconciled cash balance between the Accounting and Treasury records
was composed of unrecorded interest income and outstanding checks. They committed
that proper adjusting entries and communications will be made to ensure reconciliation of
cash balances between records. Likewise, they will exert effort to acquire supporting
documents to identify all reconciling items. Further, adjustments will be made for all
stale checks and moving forward, will ensure that stale checks would be adjusted at year-
end.

The Municipal Accountant submitted JEVs evidencing adjustment of stale checks
as identified in the above observations for evaluation and review by the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant (i) record the supplies and materials to the Inventory
account upon purchase and Expense account upon issuance thereof pursuant
to the asset method of accounting for recording purchases of supplies and
materials as prescribed under Section 114 of the Manual on the NGAS for
LGUs; (ii) review the entries made and effect the necessary adjustments, if
warranted, and (iii) ensure compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 in
recording transactions in the books of the Municipality; and
b. Municipal Accountant and the OIC-GSO maintain supplies ledger card and
supplies stock card, respectively, for each stock of inventory to account for
the issuance of its inventories to avoid possible misuse and wastage, and
conduct regular reconciliation between the Accounting and GSO records for
inventory accounts and effect the necessary adjustments, if warranted.

In a letter dated March 22, 2022, the Municipal Accountant committed that they
will exert effort to analyze the observations and provide adjusting entries as necessary.
They will also exert effort to continuously meet with the GSO to reconcile their inventory
records.

Division provided the actions that will be taken relative to the Audit Team�s
recommendations such as (i) designating personnel who will be responsible for
maintaining the property cards; and (ii) conduct a quarterly reconciliation of Inventory
Accounts with the records of the Municipal Accounting Office.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor consider the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances as
provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to come
up with a reliable PPE balance;
b. Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical count and technical
valuation of the property, plant and equipment to establish existence,
ownership and condition of the properties owned by the Municipality
especially for the local road network to determine the current value of the
road components and facilitate the preparation of the RPCLRN; and ensure
that the required details in the reports were accurately and completely filled
out;
c. Municipal Accountant and OIC-GSO allot time and effort to (i) coordinate
and prepare complete supporting schedules to facilitate the reconciliation of
the property accounts and establishment of actual existence, ownership and
condition of the properties especially the previous year�s PPE acquisitions;
(ii) prepare and maintain appropriate and required property records and
reports for all PPE accounts especially for the Road Networks; and (iii)
coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to complete the maintenance of a
road map with complete description and cost segregation of road components
for road projects;
d. OIC-GSO (i) analyze the items included in the RPCPPE to exclude non-PPE
accounts such as repair and maintenance expense and inventory, expendable
and semi-expendable properties, and properties not presented during the
physical count; (ii) prepare and maintain RLRN and Road Map to account
for the Local Roads and reconcile with the Registry of Public Infrastructures
maintained by the Accounting Office; and (iii) collate the documents of the
land owned by the Municipal Government that remained uncovered by a
Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT);
e. Municipal Accountant ensure proper classification of accounts in accordance
with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs as prescribed by COA
Circular 2015-009 dated December 01, 2015 and review the entries made
relevant to the above transactions and effect the necessary adjustments, if
warranted, and be guided by COA Circular No. 2015-009 in recording
similar transactions in the books of the Municipality; and
f. Municipal Engineer maintain a road map and provide the Municipal
Accountant and the GSO with a complete description and cost segregation of
road components for road projects.

In a letter dated March 22, 2022, the Municipal Accountant committed that they
will exert effort to analyze the observations made and provide adjusting entries as
necessary. They will also exert effort to continuously meet with GSO to reconcile their
PPE records.

In an indorsement dated March 23,2022, the Municipal General Services Office
Division provided the actions that will be taken relative to the Audit Team�s
recommendations, as follows:
a. The GSO will seek approval from the Agency Head regarding the one-time
cleansing of PPE accounts with the coordination of the Municipal Accounting
Office;
b. The GSO and Municipal Accounting Office will give ample time in the
reconciliation of their respective records and will check the entries made
pursuant to COA Circular No 2015-009;
c. The GSO will conduct a quarterly reconciliation of PPE accounts with the
Municipal Accounting Office and update the necessary records. They will also
recommend to the Agency Head to reconstitute the Municipal Local Road
Inventory Committee; and
d. The GSO will check and verify details relative to the PPE accounts. They will
also coordinate with the Municipal Engineering Office to furnish them with the
details and information about the road component of all municipal road projects
to facilitate the preparation of RPCLRN and to maintain the RLRN and Road
Map to account for the local roads and reconcile with the RPI maintained by the
Municipal Accounting Office. Further, they will check all supporting documents
relative to the lands owned by the Municipality and coordinate with the Municipal
Assessor�s Office to facilitate the titling of these lands in the name of the
Municipality.

that they have identified their current inventory of roads that needs to be revalidated.
They will take time to update the road inventory, prepare the road maps pursuant to
existing regulations and provide the same to the Municipal Accounting Office and GSO.

The Municipal Accountant submitted the JEVs and justifications on March 30,
2022 for evaluation and review by the Audit Team.

Accountant and the GSO with complete description and cost segregation of
road components for road projects.

On February 17, 2020, the GSO-Designate informed the Audit Team that the
inventory committee for the local road networks will convene this February 2020 to
speed-up the preparation of RPCLRN.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
a. Municipal Accountant ensure that expenditures of the MGP are properly
classified in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts prescribed in
COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; and
b. Municipal Budget Officer review the budget for the succeeding years
covering the lump sum appropriations of OMOE and make revision of the
budget in accordance with the results of the review following the account
description mentioned in COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1,
2015.
On February 21, 2020, the Municipal Accountant informed the Audit Team
that she issued a memorandum to the accounting personnel directing them to
maintain record of actual charges of expenditures coded by the budget in OMOE,
and to regularly coordinate and reconcile with the personnel of the Municipal Budget
Office.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer: (a) install sound
internal control on the collection and remittance by requiring the Revenue
Collectors to remit their collections daily and avoid keeping large amount of
money in their possession; (b) ensure that the collections of the collectors are
within the maximum cash accountability allowed in their fidelity bond, and if
possible, coordinate with the Bureau of Treasury for the increase of their
fidelity bonds; (c) require the LO to verify first in the logbook if all issued
accountable forms were returned to the custodian of accountable forms before
accepting remittance from the collectors; (d) require the custodian of
accountable forms to verify first if collections corresponding to the previously
issued accountable forms were already remitted before issuing another set of
accountable forms; and (e) ensure that the duplicate copies of AF 52 ,53 and 54
are attached to the RCR to verify the collections stated therein.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Mayor instructed the Acting
Municipal Treasurer to report directly to him the collectors with unremitted
collections for his appropriate action.

Also, the Acting Municipal Treasurer manifested that he devise a system that
will monitor the collections and remittance of the collectors. In addition, he ordered
the Liquidating Officer to verify the remittance vis-à-vis the record of issued
accountable officer.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
a. Municipal Accountant (i) reconcile with the records of the Audit Team and
cause the refund of the excessive payment of travelling expenses of
P46,342.00; (ii) thoroughly review and examine the claims of Municipal
officials and employees so that claims for traveling expenses either thru cash
advance or reimbursement will not result to overpayment in excess of the
rates indicated in EO No. 77; (iii) ensure that the cash advances granted to
Regular Disbursing Officer be recorded in the appropriate advances account
pursuant to COA Circular 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; and (iv) submit
the lacking documents in the liquidation of cash advances and
reimbursements of traveling expenses and allowances as provided in COA
Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; and
b. Acting Municipal Treasurer ensure that all the Special Disbursing Officer
maintain cashbook pursuant to Sections 6.1 to 6.4 of COA Circular No. 97-
002 dated February 10, 1997.

In the letter dated February 17, 2020 of the HRMO to the Municipal Mayor,
copy furnished the Audit Team, it was stated that the Human Resource Capacity
Development Plan (HRCDP) which was prepared by each Department/Office of
MGP and consolidated by HRMO, and the Executive-Legislative Agenda (ELA)
embody the MGP’s need and desire for organizational improvement and serve as the
basis for continuous hiring of additional manpower to maintain efficiency and
productivity of the Offices.

The HRMO added that they will require the requesting Head of Office to
include in their request letter the justification and the specific task of the personnel to
be hired. Also, they will require the supervisor, instead of the Municipal Mayor, to
sign the certification of service rendered in the payroll.

Moreover, in the letter of the Municipal Accountant dated February 24, 2020,
the Audit Team was informed that the issue of misclassification has been identified in
the last year’s planning activity and the corresponding corrections were reflected in
the Annual Budget for CY 2020. Further, she committed that all payments for
services rendered by job-order employees will be properly recognized in the
appropriate accounts in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the HRMO,
Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant to provide justification on the excess
payment of P239,000.00, and make reconciliation with the Audit Team to
determine the actual excess payment to be refunded by the Municipal
employees. Henceforth, require the HRMO and Municipal Budget Officer to
abide with Item 3 of CSC MC No. 42, series of 1992, as amended by CSC MC
No. 6, series of 2002, dated March 1, 2002 in the grant of loyalty pay to entitled
municipal officials and employees.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
a. Acting Municipal Treasurer and other concerned offices coordinate with the
suppliers and employees to inform them of the outstanding payables,
otherwise provide certification to the Municipal Accounting Office that there
are no valid claimants for the long outstanding payables to facilitate the
reversion of funds; and
b. Municipal Accountant (i) ensure that only obligations with approved
disbursement voucher and complete supporting documents, and delivery of
goods or services rendered be booked as accounts payable pursuant to the
prevailing rules and regulation; and (ii) analyze and conduct a thorough
review of the Accounts Payable and Due to Officers and Employees accounts
under the GF and SEF with payables outstanding for more than two years to
facilitate the reversion to the unappropriated surplus as mandated in Section
98 of PD No. 1445.

The Accounting Office has provided the Treasury Office the list of Accounts
Payable which were outstanding for more than two years for their verification and
appropriate action. Also, the Municipal Accountant issued certification of accounts
payable that were in excess of the amount claimed, and another certification for
accounts under Due to Officers and Employees which was not claimed due to lack of
supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) conduct a thorough
review and analysis of Due to NGAs and Trust Liabilities accounts; (ii) exert
effort to collate details about the fund transfers from the GF amounting to
P1,664,163.92 and the long outstanding balances included in the Trust
Liabilities; and (iii) coordinate with the implementing offices of the long
outstanding fund transfers so that appropriate actions may be undertaken
thereon.

After the analysis of the Accounting Office, the fund transfers from the BFAR
and DA pertain to projects to be implemented by the Municipal Agriculturist Office.
The Municipal Agriculturist committed to coordinate with the funding agencies for
the updating and revision of the project proposal to make the projects more feasible
for implementation.

We recommended that the:
a. MSWDO and MDRRMO (i) provide justification on the deficiencies noted in
the distribution of welfare goods during Typhoon Usman and Tisoy,
otherwise refund the unaccounted and undistributed welfare goods totaling
P805,486.00; (ii) provide the Accounting Office and the Audit Team
distribution list of the procured welfare goods out of the financial assistance
from Land Bank of the Philippines and the donated non-food item from the
Office of the Civil Defense; and (iii) ensure that welfare goods distributed
were properly accounted and tallied with the record of its procurement;
b. MDRRMO (i) provide the Municipal Accountant relevant documents of the
donated three units of generator set and chainsaw for recording in the books
of accounts and accountability purposes; and (ii) initiate the distribution of
the IEC materials to the grade 10 students to achieve its objectives;
c. BAC Chairman and BAC Secretariat provide justification on the noted
observations on the procurement of DRRM Equipment. Likewise, ensure
that relevant provision of the 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184 be meticulously
complied to avoid doubt on the procurement procedure of the MGP.

The Municipal Mayor and MDRRMO informed that there were welfare goods
that were re-packed and individually distributed to the affected constituents. They
committed to submit the list of recipients.

We recommended that the:
a. BAC ensure that (i) for every postponement of opening bids, Notice of
Postponement shall be posted in the PhilGEPS and the website of the
Procuring Entity; (ii) the Bid Evaluation Reports are properly dated in order
to determine the timeliness of evaluation process; (iii) the Invitation to Apply
for Eligibility to Bid, NOA and NTP shall be posted in the PhilGEPS; and
(iv) timeline in the conduct of post-qualification process, execution of
contract agreement, issuance of NTP, and project implementation period are
observed;
b. Municipal Engineer (i) ensure that the variation orders shall be duly
supported with the required documents in Item 1.5.a of Annex E of the
Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; (ii) consider all factors in the preparation of
the detailed engineering of the infrastructure projects to minimize
modification during the project implementation; (iii) observe consistency in
the dates of implementation period presented in the documents; and (iv)
review the computation of liquidated damages; and
c. Municipal Accountant, BAC, Municipal Engineer and concerned employees
submit justification of the observations noted above and the lacking
supporting documents.

The concerned Municipal Officials committed to submit the required
justification and lacking supporting documents.

We recommended that the:
a. BAC (i) ensure that the bidding documents are properly dated in order to
verify the validity and propriety of each of the documents submitted by the
prospective bidder; and (ii) abide with the provisions of the Revised IRR of
RA 9184 particularly Sections 34.8 in the conduct of post-qualification
process, 37.1.6 in the posting of the NOA, 37.2.1 in the posting of
performance security and execution of contract agreement, and 37.4.1 in the
issuance of NTP;
b. Municipal Engineer (i) revisit the computation of liquidated damages
imposed upon the contractor and provide the necessary documents to
substantiate the same in accordance with Item 8(1) of Annex E of 2016
Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and (ii) monitor and require the contractors
to make corrective actions on the defects noted in the Re-blocking of Road
at Barangay Cacawan and Re-blocking of Road at Barangay Palayan to
Anoling pursuant to Section 62.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and
apprise the Audit Team on the actions taken thereto;
c. Municipal Accountant (i) make adjustment in the books of accounts to
recognize the implementation of the project Installation of Potable Water
Source  Pambisan Malaki; (ii) abide with the provisions of COA Circular
No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 relative to the proper classification
of accounts, and Section 54.5 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in the
imposition of warranty securities; and (iii) demand payment or institute
appropriate action for the collection of the cost deficiency totaling
P15,716.89 from the respective contractors, as the case may be; and
d. Municipal Accountant, BAC, Municipal Engineer and concerned
employees submit the lacking supporting documents.

In a letter dated October 1, 2019, the former Municipal Engineer assured that
the contractors for the Re-blocking of Road at Brgy. Cacawan and Re-blocking of
Road at Brgys. Palayan to Anoling will make corrective action on the defects noted
by the COA-TAS.

Also, the concerned Municipal Officials and Employees committed to submit
the required justification and lacking supporting documents.

We recommended that the:
a. Local Development Council, Implementing Office and Municipal Planning
Development Coordinator (MPDC) (i) plan the timely procurement and
implementation of the identified priority development projects under the
20% DF to ensure that the completion of the PPAs will be attained as
planned and avoid duplication with projects implemented by other agencies;
(ii) coordinate with the barangays with PPAs that will be included in the
20% DF AIP in to avoid interruptions in the implementation of the barangay
development projects and ensure that the barangays will be well-informed
of their responsibilities as beneficiaries of the municipal development
projects; (iii) ensure that the recipients of the barangay development
program have a prepared fund for the counterpart in order to implement the
requested PPAs as planned; and (iv) confirm the feasibility of the
particular PPAs and observe timeliness in their implementation to achieve
the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes;
b. Municipal Engineer (i) submit the list of completed projects to the MBO and
MAccO to facilitate the reversion and re-appropriation of savings/
unexpended balances from the implementation of projects under the 20%
DF to eligible development projects; and (ii) request for additional
manpower, if necessary, to expedite the preparation of POW particularly
for barangay development projects and strictly follow the planned
implementation period as indicated in the AIP;
c. Municipal Engineer and the BAC (i) periodically provide the MBO and the
MAccO of the status of projects and the procurements undertaken by the
Municipality to assist the concerned offices in the periodic review and
monitoring of the balances of current and continuing appropriations; (ii)
immediately inform the MBO and MAccO whenever there are changes on
the awarded PPAs and problems in the project’s implementation to make
the necessary adjustment in the financial reports relative to the affected
PPAs; and (iii) provide acceptable justification on the noted observations;
and
d. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer (i) reconcile the
SAAOB and obligate those awarded and completed projects; and (ii)
provide acceptable justification on the noted observations regarding the
discrepancies of their records of 20% Development Fund.

The MPDC in his letter to the Municipal Mayor dated February 14, 2020, copy
furnished the Audit Team, committed to improve the planning, coordination and
monitoring services for efficient project implementation and completion, ideally
resulting to 100% physical accomplishment, judicious fund utilization and

Proper reconciliation of records will be undertaken after the regular meeting
scheduled every first Friday of the month.

We recommended that the:
a. MPDC, MDRRMC and MDRRM Officer provide specific, measurable,
achievable, realistic and time-bound (SMART) performance indicators in
each PPAs appropriated for MDRRM Fund to facilitate easy monitoring,
and quantify the attainment of the planned PPAs and avoid the use of fund

b. MDRM Officer and BAC Chairman monitor and facilitate the
implementation of the PPAs under the MDRRM Fund and ensure that all

which it was appropriated; and

During the exit conference, the MDRRM Officer committed to avoid inclusion

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the PESO Manager
to (i) submit justification on the observations regarding the implementation of

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Acting
Municipal Treasurer and the Market Supervisor to (a) continue to send bills and
follow-up those bills to delinquent market stallholders to attain their revenue
target and eliminate delinquencies or be kept to a minimal amount; (b) strictly
enforce the pertinent provisions of the New Revenue of the Municipality
relative to the actions of delinquencies; (c) design/adopt measures and strategies
to remedy the low collection of income and minimize the expenses in relation
to the market operations to at least break-even or lower the operating expenses;
and (d) take into consideration the concerns of the market stallholders without
sacrificing the compliance to the Revenue Code of the Municipality.

The Market Supervisor informed the Audit Team that their Office (i) assumed
two abandoned stalls and informed the Office of the Municipal Mayor on the status
of the stalls; and (ii) confiscated, with due process, two stalls that are sub-leasing.
Also, table owners who were no longer occupying the said space were advised
through a letter if they are still willing to occupy the said space, if not, the space will
be leased to other vendors.

supervisor; and (d) stop the practice where the Municipal Mayor is signing

In the letter dated February 17, 2020 of the HRMO to the Municipal Mayor,
copy furnished the Audit Team, it was stated that the Human Resource Capacity
Development Plan (HRCDP) which was prepared by each Department/Office of
MGP and consolidated by HRMO, and the Executive-Legislative Agenda (ELA)
embody the MGP’s need and desire for organizational improvement and serve as the

The HRMO added that they will require the requesting Head of Office to
include in their request letter the justification and the specific task of the personnel to
be hired. Also, they will require the supervisor, instead of the Municipal Mayor, to

We recommended that the:

identify the person responsible for the late filing and payment of DST for the

b. Acting Municipal Treasurer (i) trace the unidentified and unremitted tax

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the GAD Focal
Person:
a. Prioritize the attendance of the GFPS Executive Committee, TWG and MET
to various GAD-related trainings and seminars in order for them to serve as
a catalyst in gender mainstreaming in the Municipality and identify gender
issues in their GPB;
b. Instruct the MPDC to maintain database with sex-disaggregated data and
other relevant data that will serve as basis for gender-responsive planning,
programming and policy formulation, and aid in identifying gender issues;
c. Instruct the GFPS to prioritize identifying gender issue that is focus on the
need of the clients;
d. Instruct the GFPS-TWG to submit the DILG-received GAD AR and its
supporting documents to facilitate evaluation of the success of the GPB for
CY 2019; and
e. Instruct the lead offices to prepare the implementing rules and regulation of
the CY 2016 GAD Code of the Municipality to effectively implement the
provisions contained therein.

On February 14, 2020, the GAD Focal Person informed that in order to
capacitate the GFPS a proposed capacity building, which includes gender-sensitivity
training (GST) and use of GAD tools, shall be undertaken in CY 2020.

The MPDC in his letter dated February 14, 2020 informed that they designated
one regular employee as GAD Database Focal assisted by one contract of service
personnel to ensure continuous development and maintenance of GAD database.
Further, the MPDC mentioned that they are exerting efforts to enhance the fillings in
GAD database by way of gathering data and information related to the purpose. They
will (i) request data of various government non-government agencies, and (ii) ask the
HRMO to provide them sex-disaggregated data about the manpower or personnel of
the MGP and the assistance of the DILG in establishing an effectual GAD
monitoring system as a way of tracking the progress of project implementation.

The GAD Focal Person submitted the GAD AR for CY 2019 without any
supporting documents and acknowledgement from the DILG.

instruct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to enforce the

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the (a) Municipal Accountant and Treasurer
reconcile their cash records and settle the disparity in the balances of cash accounts
per GL and cashbooks; (b) Municipal Treasurer maintain and update cashbooks of
all bank accounts to monitor daily transactions; and (c) Municipal Accountant (i)
submit the bank reconciliation statements or status report for time deposit/BIR
EFPS for BIR-EFPS and HYSA account for the period January to December 2018;
(ii) ensure immediate preparation of all bank reconciliation statements for all bank
accounts; (iii) conduct detailed review and checking of bank records/reports and
request for the necessary documents to support the recording of the reconciling
items, if any; (iv) exert effort to substantiate the identified reconciling items and
after which prepare the necessary adjustments; and (v) review the long
outstanding/stale checks specifically those checks without identified payees, and
require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnish
the Accountant with the original DVs and its supporting documents, for the
preparation of the JEV following the procedures prescribed under the MNGAS for
LGUs to ensure recording of such cancellation.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Accountant and General Service
Officer allot time and effort to: (i) coordinate and prepare supporting schedules to
facilitate the proper reconciliation of the property accounts and establishment of
actual existence, ownership and condition of the properties; and (ii) prepare and
maintain appropriate property records and reports for PPE account; (b)Municipal
Accountant to analyze the nature of transactions and make an adjustment to
properly classify the items to an appropriate PPE account; (c) GSO to:(i) remove
the expendable and semi-expendable properties from the RPCPPE and account all
determined unserviceable properties to facilitate the preparation of Inventory and
Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and other required
supporting documents for disposal; (ii) prepare and maintain RLRN and Road Map
to account for the Local Roads and reconcile with the Registry of Public
Infrastructures maintained by the Accounting Office; (d) Inventory Committee
conduct a complete physical count and technical valuation of the local road network
to determine the current value of the road components to facilitate the preparation
of the RPCLRN; and (e) Municipal Engineer to maintain road map and provide the
Municipal Accountant and the GSO with complete description and cost segregation
of road components for road projects.

We recommended that the (a) BAC Chairman and Municipal Accountant
submit acceptable justification on the delay in the execution of contracts in violation
of Section 37.2.1 of Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and submit the lacking supporting
documents for audit purposes; (b) all concerned officials to work hand-in-hand in
the prompt implementation and documentation of the projects; (c) BAC Chairman
provide explanation on the inconsistent dates noted in the supporting documents;
(d) BAC Chairman, Municipal Engineer, Zoning Officer and Municipal Accountant
submit satisfactory justification on the delayed implementation and payment of the
projects; (e) Municipal Zoning Officer abide by the pertinent provisions of No.
2010-101 dated September 23,2010 regarding the documentation of the municipal
vehicles; and (f) Municipal Engineer institute corrective actions on the destroyed
open dumpsite to prevent further destruction of the project.

We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Engineer (i) provide acceptable
justification in the grant of suspension of work for the SALINTUBIG Projects; (ii)
provide explanation on the delay of the submission of DED to DILG which was not
in accordance with the provisions of the Memorandum Agreement; (iii) institute
action to remedy the non-potability of the water derived from the SALINTUBIG
Projects; (iv) provide justification on the alteration of the SALINTUBIG project in
barangay Papandayan; and (v) inspect and institute corrective action, if any is
needed relative to the eroding concrete finishing of the Seawall in barangay
Papandayan; (b) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer revisit the
computation of liquidated damages imposed upon the contractors/supplier and
provide the necessary documents to substantiate the same in accordance with Item
8(1) of Annex E of Revised IRR of RA No.9184, and demand the same for payment;
(c) BAC Chairman thru the BAC Secretariat provide explanation on the delay on
the posting of NOA in PhilGEPS and the non-compliance with Sections 37.2.1, 39.1,
and 37.4.1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and (d) Municipal Accountant (i)
provide acceptable justification on the uncertainty of the information in the paid
DVs and its supporting documents; and (ii) abide strictly with the provisions of
COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 relative to the proper
classification of accounts; and (e) Municipal General Service Officer (GSO) to
prepare property sticker for the shredding machine similar to the design and
specifications set under DILG MC No. 2017-160 dated November 29, 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the HRMO to submit
the service records of the municipal employees or certification of the number of
government service of each employees who were granted Loyalty Award for further
verification of the Audit Team of the entitlement of the municipal employees of the
award given.

We recommended that: (a) In the planning stage, the Local Development
Council, Implementing Office and Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator (i) consider various factors, such as urgency of the project and weather
factor in stating the period of implementation of the programs/projects/activities;
(ii) evaluate viability of the projects as well as the need for any additional fund
needed to complete a project programmed in the previous year; and (iii) ensure that
the recipients of the Barangay Development Program has adequate funding to
provide the necessary counterpart for the project; (b)the Municipal Engineer (i)
periodically provide the status of projects undertaken by the Municipality as basis
of the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant in their periodic review
and monitoring of balances of continuing appropriations; (ii) work hand-in-hand in
providing reports to reflect the true status of the projects under the 20%
Development project; and (iii) fast track the preparation of POW and strictly follow
the planned implementation period as per the duly approved Annual Investment
Plan; and (c) Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer obligate those awarded
projects.

We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal
Accountant and MDRRMO provide a detailed list or specific description of the
PPAs under the continuing appropriation; (b) Municipal Accountant ensures that
all valid obligations of MDRRM Fund be recorded in the books of accounts to
properly reflect the accurate utilization rate of MDRRM Fund; and (c) MDRRMO,
Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairman fast-tract the implementation of the PPAs
under the MDRRM Fund by monitoring the period for which the PPAs will be
implemented. Henceforth, ensure that all projects appropriated in MDRRM Fund
be implemented during the year it was appropriated in order to increase the
capacity of the Municipality to cope with potential hazards and disasters.

We recommended that the MSWDO (a) train and assist the OSCA on the
proper use of each of the physical fitness equipment in order to attain the objectives
of acquiring the equipment, (b) prepare instructions on how to use and operate each
equipment as well as the precautionary measures in using them so that the senior
citizens will be safe while using the equipment,(c) inform all the senior citizens
regarding the additional benefits and privileges given to the them by the
government which includes the enjoyment of using the physical fitness equipment
(d) return the borrowed foot massage; and (e) consider using the physical fitness
equipment stored in the OSCA and MSWDO storage room during the Senior
Citizen’s Week celebration for the effective utilization of the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC, MENRO-
Designate, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Budget Officer to make a concrete
plan for the power supply requirement of the biodegradable shredding machine in
order to fully utilize the said machine and achieve the objectives set thereto.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Acting Municipal
Treasurer and the Market Supervisor; (a) to send bills and follow-up those bills to
delinquent market stallholders to attain their revenue target and delinquencies be
eliminated or be kept to a minimal amount; (b) strictly enforce the pertinent
provisions of the New Revenue of the Municipality relative to the actions of
delinquencies; (c) and take into consideration the concerns of the market
stallholders without sacrificing the compliance to the Revenue Code of the
Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor in the subsequent years require
the Municipal Engineer to prepare the monthly monitoring report of the awarded
infra projects, programs and activities, as well as the list of Ongoing Projects,
Programs and Activities, and those that are to be implemented during the year in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that the GAD-TWG (a) attend the GAD planning and
budgeting training; and (b) strictly comply with Section 36 of RA No. 9710 and
PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-001 to ensure that the PPAs are aligned
with GAD-related undertakings to assure the intended beneficiaries of the services,
protection, and other assistance that may be provided thru GAD PPAs.

Be that as it may, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant ensure that
government share for contributions in GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth be recorded
in the appropriate liability account and be supported by a subsidiary ledger.

We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Accountant (i) ensure timely preparation and submission of the bank reconciliation statements, (ii) analyze the reconciling items identified in the bank reconciliation statements and make necessary adjustments pertaining thereto in the period in which it is identified; (iii) exert extra effort to review the long outstanding/stale checks specifically those checks without identified payees, and (iv) require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the Accountant with the original DVs and its supporting documents, for the preparation of the JEV following the procedures prescribed under the MNGAS for LGUs to ensure recording of such cancellation; and (b) The Municipal Treasurer make representation with the concerned depository bank regarding the net reconciling items that needs adjustment in the bank records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to submit the bank reconciliation statements, however, failed to adjust reconciling items.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to do the adjustment of the noted reconciling items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Officials and Employees refund the excessive cash advance totaling P52,670.00 to avoid disallowance in audit. Henceforth, comply with the provision of Section 4 of EO No. 298 dated March 23, 2004 in preparing the claim for traveling expenses; and (b) Municipal Accountant consider the disbursement of financial assistance of the Office of the Municipal Mayor thru a revolving fund subject to replenishment. Likewise, provide explanations on the observations noted and submit the lacking documents in the liquidation of cash advances of the SDO.
observations.managementAction: The concerned Municipal Officials and Employees refunded the excess in cash advance of P52,670.00, and compliance with EO 298 was ensured by the Municipal Accountant. Documents were submitted by the SDO.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the:(a) Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate assign one staff to maintain complete ledger and stock or property cards for each category of assets and undertake the reconciliation of the result of the physical count of PPE with their respective records for the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements;(b) Municipal Accountant trace all records of Public Infrastructure which were previously closed to government equity so the Road Network and other public infrastructure be recorded back in compliance with the provision of Item 3.7 of COA Circular 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016; and (c) designated property custodians and Inventory Team initially conduct the physical count of property and equipment in every office to facilitate the complete inventory of all property and equipment under the General and Special Education Funds, and submit the Inventory Report to the Municipal Accountant and to the Office of the Auditor on or before January 31 of each year.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee composed of the personnel from Office of the Municipal Mayor, personnel from GSO, and designated Supply Officer of each Department, was able to conduct and inventory of PPE of the Municipality pursuant to Memorandum No. MO 034-2018 of the Municipal Mayor on November 16, 2018.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower and knowledge of relevant rules and regulations on the preparation of Property Cards, Property Ledger Cards, RPCLRN and available records

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate maintain real property ledger cards and real property cards, respectively, for them to monitor the real properties owned by Municipality; (b) Municipal Assessor and GSO-Designate (i) explain the observations noted above; (ii) collate all the necessary documents for the titling of parcels of land which are still in the name of the previous owner; and (c) Municipal Budget Officer annually appropriate funds on a per parcel basis to ensure that the Municipality’s budget can accommodate the survey and titling of each land in the name of the Municipality to preserve legal rights over the real properties and to avoid exposure to third party claims.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee composed of the personnel from Office of the Municipal Mayor, personnel from GSO, and designated Supply Officer of each Department, was able to conduct and inventory of PPE of the Municipality pursuant to Memorandum No. MO 034-2018 of the Municipal Mayor on November 16, 2018.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower and knowledge of relevant rules and regulations on the preparation of Property Cards, Property Ledger Cards, RPCLRN and available records

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Accountant record the construction/installation of the transformer at the municipal compound to expense account or appropriate repair and maintenance account in conformity with the new chart of accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 and submit the lacking documents to avoid suspension in audit; (b) BAC Chairman explains why the Purchase Order was issued to the winning supplier even without valid performance security. Hereafter, ensures that performance security is posted by the winning supplier before entering into a contract agreement consistent with the provision on Section 37.2.1 of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184; and (c) The Municipal Engineer submit satisfactory explanations why the transformers were not installed despite having the materials and labor to implement the same.
observations.managementAction: Prepared JEV for the reclassification of the transformer from Power Supply System to PYA. Management were able to submit lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairman and Municipal Accountant submit satisfactory explanation on the noted observations in the procurement of 1-unit ambulance, 4-units motorcycles and 1-unit second-hand multi-cab, and submit all the lacking documents to avoid suspension in audit. Henceforth, ensure that performance security is already posted prior to entering into a contract agreement; ensure compliance with the provision of Section 74 of COA Circular 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and the Municipal Accountant certify availability of funds pursuant to Section 86 of PD 1445; and ensure the attachment of complete supporting documents in the DV.
observations.managementAction: Management were able to provide justification on the noted observations and submitted some of the lacking documents.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to collate the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairman, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant submit satisfactory explanation on the observations noted and submit all the lacking documents to avoid suspension in audit; ensure strict compliance with RA 9184, Sections 62.1, 37.2.1, 39.1, 37.4.1 among others; and the Municipal Accountant require New Emrian Hardware for the payment of liquidated damages of P32,833.28 for delay in delivery of the materials for the construction of Evacuation Center, Phase III.
observations.managementAction: Liquidated damages of P32,833.28 had been paid and some of the lacking documents were submitted.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to collate the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (a) Municipal Accountant, BAC Chairman, Municipal Engineer, HRMO, and Municipal Agriculturist (i) explain the observations noted in the procurement and implementation of the P5,090,330.00 developmental projects, and (ii) submit all the lacking documents to avoid suspension in audit.; and (b) Municipal Accountant require New Emrian Hardware for the payment of liquidated damages of P32,833.28 for delay in delivery of the materials for the construction of Evacuation Center, Phase III.
observations.managementAction: Justification and some of the lacking documents were submitted.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to collate the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management refrain from affixing any picture, image, motto, logo, color motif, initials or other symbol or graphic representation associated with the top leadership of the project proponent or implementing agency and strictly adhere to the provisions cited in the COA Circular No. 2013-004 to avoid future disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The amount was refunded by the Municipal Mayor on September 25, 2018 under OR#9857833.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Mayor require the concerned department heads to explain the incurrence of unnecessary and excessive expenditures to avoid disallowance in audit. Hence, stop the practice of purchasing food items for personal consumption of the employees and ensure that other supplies to be procured are within reasonable amount and quantity in order to finance other priority project of the Municipality.; and (b) HRMO and Municipal Accountant submit all the lacking documents to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: Submitted acceptable justification and the lacking supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Mayor and Officials(i) provide acceptable justification on the observations noted above; (ii) observe strictly the austerity measures set by DILG and avoid the conduct of benchmarking activity, and assessment and planning to places outside of the province, instead, appropriate funds to be programs/projects/activities that will bring immediate socio-economic benefits among its local constituents; and (b) HRMO and Municipal Accountant submit all the lacking documents to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor and Officials(i) provide acceptable justification on the observations noted above; (ii) observe strictly the austerity measures set by DILG and avoid the conduct of benchmarking activity, and assessment and planning to places outside of the province, instead, appropriate funds to be programs/projects/activities that will bring immediate socio-economic benefits among its local constituents; and (b) HRMO and Municipal Accountant submit all the lacking documents to avoid suspension in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Budget Officer after checking the consistency of the submitted Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) of each offices, forward the same to the BAC Secretariat for their consolidation and preparation of indicative Annual Procurement Plan (APP); (b) BAC Chairman abide with the provision of Section 12.1 of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184 by implementing only the staff of the BAC Office in the conduct of procurement activities of the Municipality; (c) BAC and GSO consider the procurement of common-use supplies in the Procurement Service of the Department of Budget and Management (PS-DBM) or  bulk purchases to avail of volume discount and lesser paper work and travelling cost; and (d) Municipal Accountant explain why the purchase orders for common-use supplies were not certified by her as to funds availability pursuant to Section 86 of PD 1445. Henceforth, ensure that she certified the availability of funds prior to the execution of purchase orders/contract agreements to be entered by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer after checking the consistency of the submitted Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) of each offices, forward the same to the BAC Secretariat for their consolidation and preparation of indicative Annual Procurement Plan (APP); BAC Chairman abide with the provision of Section 12.1 of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184 by implementing only the staff of the BAC Office in the conduct of procurement activities of the Municipality; BAC and GSO consider the procurement of common-use supplies in the Procurement Service of the Department of Budget and Management (PS-DBM) or  bulk purchases to avail of volume discount and lesser paper work and travelling cost; and (d) Municipal Accountant explain why the purchase orders for common-use supplies were not certified by her as to funds availability pursuant to Section 86 of PD 1445. Henceforth, ensure that she certified the availability of funds prior to the execution of purchase orders/contract agreements to be entered by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) MSWDO abide the provision of DSWD Department Order No. 01, series of 2011 in granting Assistance to Individual in Crisis Situation (AICS) and prepare guidelines on the amount of assistance to be granted to each individual for different level cases; and (b) Municipal Accountant (i) explain the noted deficiencies in the misclassified accounts, and (ii) require the liquidation of those financial assistance transferred to other municipalities, barangays, national government agencies and private organizations to have fair presentation of financial statement. Hereafter, ensure proper classification of the assistance granted in accordance with the prescribed policy.
observations.managementAction: Prepared JEV for the reclassification of the transformer from Power Supply System to PYA. Management were able to submit lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the: (a) Municipal Budget Officer allocate the required 1% of their IRA for LCPC programs/projects/activities as mandated by Section 15 of RA 9344; and (b) The MSWDO abide with the rules and regulations with regards to the formulation of budget and utilization of the 1% LCPC fund as provided by DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Allocation was made for the LCPC.
observations.reason: Lack of knowledge of relevant rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Budget Officer and MSWDO provide a detailed list or specific description of the PPAs under the continuing appropriation. Henceforth, ensure that the lump-sum appropriation in the 70% Mitigating Fund is provided with the detailed information of all the PPAs to be funded out of the MDRRMF; and (b) if it is the policy of the Municipal Budget Officer to wait for the review of the Sangguniang Panlalawigan before obligating capital outlays, the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) may commence with the project procurement short of award until they receive the review of their budget; (c) the Municipal Engineer periodically provide the status of projects undertaken by the Municipality as basis of the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant in their periodic review and monitoring of balances of continuing appropriations; and (d) the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant review and analyze the unexpended balances of appropriations of completed projects for reversion to the unappropriated surplus in accordance with Section 322 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Submitted photocopies of official receipts evidencing refund of unused DILG funds and other lacking documents.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to collate the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: (a) The Municipal Accountant to analyze the recorded Road Network then prepare a subsidiary record for each component of the road network and Local Road Network Ledger Card. Likewise, revisit the recorded accumulated depreciation and prepare lapsing schedule showing how the accumulated depreciation was computed; (b) The GSO-Designate to maintain Local Road Inventory Road Map and Local Road Network Property Card. Moreover, conduct physical count of the road network and prepare Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN) and submit the same to this office not later than January 31 of each year; and (c) The Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO –Designate with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer has not provided the cost segregation of the road components for road projects to the Municipal Accountant. However some information were submitted to the Audit Team but insufficient to perform validation.
observations.reason: Cost segregation of the local road networks were still not provided by the Municipal Engineer. Likewise, due to limited number of personnel of accounting and GSO, required cards were not maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: The Municipal Treasurer to stop issuing accountable forms to erring accountable officers in order to avoid the practice of keeping collections in their hands for a long period of time; Install control over the issuance of cash ticket and its remittance, by designating an employee independent from Municipal Treasurer’s Office and Market Department that will randomly check the issuance of cash tickets made by the Assistant Municipal Treasurer and its remittance to the Liquidating Officer; The Assistant Municipal Treasurer to refrain from issuing cash tickets to non-bonded employees pursuant to Section 101 of PD 1445, Section 305(f) of RA 7160, Section 50, Chapter 9, Subtitle B, Book V, EO No 292 and Bureau of Treasury, Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 5, 2009. The Liquidating Officer (Cashier II) to stop keeping large amount of collections in her hand to avoid possible loss and misappropriation. Likewise, practice depositing the collections intact everyday regardless of the amount since the Municipal Treasurer’s Office is only less than 1 km away from their AGDB.
observations.managementAction: Collections were ensured to be deposited at the end of the day or the next banking day. Only bonded employees were given accountable forms. The Assistant Municipal Treasurer refrained from issuing cash tickets to non-bonded employees and only bonded employees were assigned in the collecting function.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to monitor the issuance of cash tickets.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant exhaust all the possible remedies provided under existing rules and regulations to enforce full settlement/liquidation of cash advances and collection of receivables outstanding as of December 31, 2016. If the effort is vain, the Municipal Mayor may file a request for write-off to the Commission on Audit, taking into consideration the guidelines provided by COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Refunded the cash advances and other receivables totalling P11,815.54

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the supplies and materials to Inventory account for purchase of supplies and materials as prescribed under Section 114 of the Manual for NGAS-LGU. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate maintain supplies ledger card and supplies stock card, respectively, for each stock of inventory to properly account the issuance of its inventories to avoid possible misuse and wastage. Henceforth, conduct physical count of inventories and prepare Report of Physical Inventories, and furnish the COA Auditor of such report.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is using inventory account for purchase of supplies. And conduct inventory taking per office of the remaining supplies, medicines and other materials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: The HRMO to explain why the scope and nature of work of the job-order workers were not included in the plantilla of contractual appointment; The Municipal Accountant explain why no retention was made on the payment of materials in consonance with the provision of Section 62.1 of IRR of RA 9184; The MPDC, HRMO, BAC Chairman and the Municipal Engineer to explain the noted observations about the procurement and implementation of the projects; and The Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairman to submit all the lacking documents pertaining to the procurement of materials, fuel and labor to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: No appointment is prepared without PDS submitted by each laborer. Thorough verification is conducted before appointment is prepared for project-based laborers. Submit lacking documents.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to collate the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: The Municipal Accountant to explain why no retention was made on the payment of materials in consonance with the provision of Section 62.1 of IRR of RA 9184; The MPDC, HRMO, BAC Chairman and the Municipal Engineer to explain the above noted observations about the procurement and implementation of the projects; The Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairman to submit all the lacking documents pertaining to the procurement of materials, fuel and labor to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: No appointment is prepared without PDS submitted by each laborer. Thorough verification is conducted before appointment is prepared for project-based laborers. Submit lacking documents.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to collate the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: The HRMO to explain why the scope and nature of work of the job-order workers were not included in the plantilla of contractual appointment; The Municipal Accountant to explain why the LGU counterpart for the project was not transferred to Trust Fund account; The MPDC, BAC Chairman and the Municipal Engineer to explain the above noted observations about the procurement and implementation of the projects; and The Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairman to submit all the lacking documents pertaining to the procurement of materials and labor for both projects to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: No appointment is prepared without PDS submitted by each laborer. Thorough verification is conducted before appointment is prepared for project-based laborers. Submit some lacking documents.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to collate the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: The Municipal Treasurer to explain non-compliance with Section 3.1 of the Memorandum of Agreement between the DILG and the Municipality; The HRMO to explain why the scope and nature of work of the job-order workers were not included in the plantilla of contractual appointment; The Municipal Accountant explain why no retention was made on the payment of materials in consonance with the provision of Section 62.1 of IRR of RA 9184; and The MPDC, HRMO, BAC Chairman and the Municipal Engineer to explain the noted observations about the procurement and implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: No appointment is prepared without PDS submitted by each laborer. Thorough verification is conducted before appointment is prepared for project-based laborers. Submit lacking documents.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to collate the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to refrain from making payment in excess of the fund received; The HRMO to explain why the scope and nature of work of the job-order workers were not included in the plantilla of contractual appointment; and The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to submit the lacking documents pertaining the procurement of materials, fuel and labor to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: The concerned implementing office to provide copy of the signed and notarized memorandum of agreement (MOA) between the Municipality and DSWD; Submit lacking documents. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to refrain from making payment in excess of the fund received.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to collate the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairman and the Municipal Accountant submit all the lacking documents pertaining to the purchased of drugs and medicines totaling P768,210.00 to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: Submitted some of the lacking documents.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to collate the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned persons liable to the payment of legal services refund the legal fees since the hiring of private lawyer was without prior written authority from the Office of the Solicitor General with the concurrence of the Commission on Audit in compliance with the provision of COA Circular No. 98-002 dated June 9, 1998.
observations.managementAction: Submitted justification on the hiring of lawyer, which was handling legal cases in the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Management strictly conforms to CSC rule regarding the granting of awards and incentives under PRAISE; (b) Municipal employees concerned refund the cash awards and cost of food items amounting to P522,000.00 and P1,165,500.00, respectively due to lack of legal basis and supporting documents; and (c) The BAC strictly adheres to the provisions of Revised IRR of RA 9184 for the procurement of goods.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted the PRAISE program approved on 2001, and no amendments was made thereafter. Municipal employees concerned refunded the cash awards and cost of food items amounting to P522,000.00 and P1,165,500.00, respectively. The BAC strictly adheres to the provisions of Revised IRR of RA 9184 for the procurement of goods.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer: Instruct all collectors especially those inside the Treasurer’s Office to remit their collections intact and daily before the close of office hours to the Liquidating Officer for turn-over to the Municipal Treasurer pursuant to Section 31 of Manual on New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), Volume I; deposit, intact all collections on the same day or the next banking day to minimize exposure to risk of possible loss pursuant to Section 32 of MNGAS, Volume I; Ensure that only bonded employees will be assigned in collecting function of the Treasurer’s Office pursuant to the provision of Section 101 of PD 1445, Section 305(f) of RA 7160; Section 50, Chapter 9, Subtitle B, Book V, Executive Order 2092 and Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 5, 2009; and Closely supervise the collecting functions assigned to the Job-Order.
observations.managementAction: Remittance was made on the day collections were made, and deposited at the end of banking or the next banking day. Only bonded employees were assigned in collecting function.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: The Management considers the propriety of enacting local internal guidelines providing for the specific criteria, administrative procedures, and standards on the grant of Magna Carta benefits to all entitled PHWs. The Accounting Personnel in charge in the preparation of payroll consider the actual exposure of public health workers to high and low risk hazards in preparing the claim of hazard pay pursuant to No. 7.2 of DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2012 dated November 29, 2012; and The Municipal Accountant submit the documents supporting the paid payrolls for CY 2014 of the Municipal Health Office in order to evaluate the propriety of the full grant of these allowances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality created internal guidelines for the granting of Magna Carta to Public Health Workers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the concerned officials and employees to submit to the COA Audit Team the documents prescribed under Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Budget Circular (BC) No. 10 dated March 29, 1996 and Item 5.10 of COA Circular 2012-001, Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions for the overtime claimed for CY 2014 to avoid suspension in audit those transactions.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the required documents except for quantified overtime accomplishment duly signed by the employee and the supervisor.
observations.reason: No comment/justification was given by Management.

We recommended that the BAC Chairman and Municipal Accountant
submit satisfactory explanation on the noted observations in the procurement of 1
unit ambulance, 4 units motorcycles and 1 unit second-hand multi-cab, and submit

We recommended and management agreed that the: (a) BAC Chairman,
Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant submit satisfactory explanation on
the observations noted and submit all the lacking documents to avoid suspension in

specifically the issuance of Notice of Award and its posting in PhilGEPS, etc.,

COA Circular 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, inclusion of complete details in PO

Designate ensures that all defective units were communicated with the supplier for

reference in conducting physical inventory taking.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct thorough review
and analysis of Due to NGAs and Trust Liabilities account, and refund the excess
amount of P39,553.55 from the fully implemented projects to the source agencies
pursuant to Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Mayor explains why the MOA was

(f) Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairman explain the

in audit.

We recommended that the: (a) MPDC and Municipal Engineer provide

Henceforth, we recommended that Management evaluate and determine

We recommended that the BAC Chairman and Municipal Accountant
explain the above noted observations. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant submit

We recommended that the: (a) BAC Chairman and Municipal Accountant

We recommended that the: (a) Administrative Officer V of Slaughterhouse,

cellphone.

84
Management (PS-DBM) or bulk purchases to avail of volume discount and lesser

PPAs are specified and not in lump-sum amount; (b) if it is the policy of the

commence with the project procurement short of award until they receive the

projects undertaken by the Municipality as basis of the Municipal Budget Officer
and Municipal Accountant in their periodic review and monitoring of balances of
continuing appropriations, submit a list of all completed projects to support the
reversion of such unexpended balance to the unappropriated surplus; and (d) the
Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant review and analyze the
unexpended balances of appropriations of completed projects for reversion to the
unappropriated surplus in accordance with Section 322 of RA 7160.

We recommended and management agreed that the:(a)Acting Municipal

observations.recommendation:

Further, the GSO-Designate may begin to maintain property records based on the deliveries of supplies or property for CY 2016 to date on the basis of the AIR and other supporting documents.
